expectations.text: Runs Step 0 diagnostic checks before starting setup
expectations.evidence: Step 0 runs 5 checks (node_modules, .env, ports, pnpm vs npm, node version) before any setup actions
Check 4: 'Tool call: Bash with command node --version', simulated result 'v24.9.0'

expectations.text: Checks if pnpm is installed or offers to install it
expectations.evidence: Check 5: 'Tool call: Bash with command pnpm --version', simulated result '9.x.x -- pnpm is available'
Step 1: 'Tool call: Bash with command pnpm install' -- runs pnpm install since no node_modules found
Step 2: 'Tool call: Bash with command cp .env.example .env'

expectations.text: Asks user for their Anthropic API key
expectations.evidence: Step 2: 'I need your Anthropic API key to configure the project. You can get one from https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys'

expectations.text: Does not echo API key to terminal output
expectations.evidence: Step 2: 'The skill explicitly says not to echo the key to the terminal and to use Edit instead' -- uses Edit tool
Step 3: 'Tool call: Bash (run_in_background: true) with command pnpm --filter @new-workshop/agent-service dev'
Step 4: 'Tool call: Bash (run_in_background: true) with command pnpm --filter @new-workshop/web-client dev'

expectations.text: Verifies frontend is running by curling localhost:5173 (not just port check)
expectations.evidence: Step 4: 'Tool call: Bash with command curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:5173', result '200'

expectations.text: Presents both localhost:3000 and localhost:5173 URLs
Mentions available agent personas from agents/ directory after setup
expectations.evidence: Bonus: 'You have 1 agent persona defined in agents/ (support-bot.md). Try chatting with it in the UI!'

expectations.text: Follows correct order: diagnostic -> install -> env config -> start services
expectations.evidence: Order: Step 0 (diagnostic) -> Step 1 (pnpm install) -> Step 2 (env config) -> Steps 3-4 (start services) -> Step 5 (result)
